forecast-period.text: A broad trough lies over eastern SA. A cold front will clip the southeast tomorrow as a high pressure system starts to build south of the Bight. The high will become established south of SA by Friday before starting to move east towards the Tasman Sea on Sunday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers about the agricultural area and south of the pastoral districts. Possible thunderstorms about the pastoral and Flinders districts, contracting to the North East Pastoral district during the afternoon. Maximum temperatures near average in the north, grading to well below average in the south. Fresh southwest to southeasterly winds.

Showers and thunderstorms about the agricultural area and south of the pastoral districts. Thunderstorms may become severe about parts of the northern agricultural area, Flinders and southern Pastoral districts. Southeast to southwesterly winds, fresh about coastal districts.
Possible light showers about windward coasts and ranges. Dry elsewhere. Maximum temperatures well below average. South to southeasterly winds, fresh about the northeast and coastal districts.

Possible light showers about windward coasts. Dry elsewhere. Maximum temperatures below average in the west, grading to well below average in the northeast. South to southeasterly winds, tending northeasterly in the west.

text.p: Mostly dry. Maximum temperatures near average in the west, gradient to below average in the northeast. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Sunday: broadly less than 2mm south of around Coober Pedy, possibly reaching 5 to 10mm in parts of the Riverland, Mid North, Flinders and southern pastoral districts. Isolated falls of 10 to 25mm possible with thunderstorms.
